events.title: Could Iran become America's next 'forever war'?
events.description: The ceasefire has collapsed, attacks are escalating and shipping in the Strait of Hormuz remains under threat. Is the US drifting into another open-ended conflict with no clear way out?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Could Iran become America's next 'forever war'?

events.title: Israel's new exhibit exposes years of Hamas terrorist planning before Oct 7 massacre
events.description: A 600-square-meter exhibition features Hamas tunnel replicas, attack vehicles, and documents revealing Hezbollah and Iran's roles in the Oct. 7 assault.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israel's new exhibit exposes years of Hamas terrorist planning before Oct 7 massacre

events.title: Australia to warn China it won’t be bullied by ‘provocative‘ actions as it builds military and nuclear arsenal
events.description: Foreign minister Penny Wong will deliver speech criticising China’s recent launch of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Penny Wong will warn China that Australia and other neighbouring nations will not be bullied by its “provocative” actions, after its involvement in another “destabilising and dangerous” incident this week. The foreign affairs minister – who will deliver the address on Thursday in the Philippines, where the 59th Asean foreign minister’s meeting is being held – will also caution world leaders against placing a toll on the strait of Hormuz after both Iran and the US threatened to do amid the protracted conflict. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia to warn China it won’t be bullied by ‘provocative‘ actions as it builds military and nuclear arsenal

events.title: Ukraine: Deadly Russian attacks severely damage aid warehouses
events.description: Russian attacks and drone strikes killed at least two children and injured dozens more across Ukraine over recent days while severely damaging three critical aid warehouses, UN agencies reported on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Deadly Russian attacks severely damage aid warehouses

events.title: World News in Brief: Sexual violence in DR Congo, deaths in Ukraine and Russia, crime in Southeast Asia
events.description: Independent UN human rights experts on Tuesday condemned the M23 armed group’s “reign of terror” over territory it controls in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), calling for an end to the Rwanda-backed group’s sexual torture and violence against civilians.
events.ticker_label: 💥 World News in Brief: Sexual violence in DR Congo, deaths in Ukraine and Russia, crime in Southeast Asia

events.title: Deadly Afghanistan floods kill at least 26 as aid agencies race to reach survivors
events.description: Flash floods have killed at least 26 people across eastern Afghanistan, including 23 in the mountainous province of Nuristan, where homes, markets and roads were swept away and search teams continued looking for people reported missing.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Deadly Afghanistan floods kill at least 26 as aid agencies race to reach survivors

events.title: DR Congo's Ebola outbreak becomes fastest ever recorded
events.description: The number of deaths in the current Ebola epidemic in the DRC has now topped 1,000, and the number of confirmed cases is around 2500. Experts are calling for more funding to help tackle the crisis as it becomes the fastest spreading outbreak ever recorded.

events.title: Spain battles spreading wildfires fuelled by climate-change driven heat
events.description: Wildfires in Spain have forced authorities to evacuate several districts around the city of Toledo, 75 miles from the Spanish capital Madrid. It's one of many wildfires ravaging across western Europe as parts of southern France and Italy are also ablaze. In 2025, more than 393,000 hectares had been consumed by flames in Spain, according to the European Forest Fire Information System. It is the worst toll in the country's recent history, fuelled by extreme temperatures cause by climate change.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Spain battles spreading wildfires fuelled by climate-change driven heat

events.title: World News in Brief: Aid for Ukraine, drone attacks in Sudan, DR Congo deaths, neurological disorders in the Americas
events.description: UN partners launched the Winter Response Plan to help 2.1 million people in Ukraine survive another cold season as the country grapples with the on-going Russian invasion.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Aid for Ukraine, drone attacks in Sudan, DR Congo deaths, neurological disorders in the Americas

events.title: Iran launches missiles at Jordan
events.description: Missile alert sirens have sounded in Jordan’s port city of Aqaba as attacks across the Middle East overshadowed diplomatic efforts in Pakistan to salvage an interim ceasefire deal that has collapsed
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran launches missiles at Jordan

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Spotlight on Syria as Guterres heads to Damascus
events.description: The Security Council discussed the situation on the ground in Syria amid reports of an Israeli attack in occupied territory and of a multi-national oil pipeline to counter the closure of the critical Strait of Hormuz – still the focus of the ongoing US-Iran war. The meeting follows UN independent investigators’ findings that security conditions remain fragile in the south as António Guterres heads to Damascus for his first visit to the country as Secretary-General.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Spotlight on Syria as Guterres heads to Damascus

events.title: How Houthi strikes on oil tankers will impact the Middle East war
events.description: Yemen's Houthi rebels say they have attacked two Saudi oil tankers. The Houthis are supported by Iran, and the attacks in the Red Sea could be a new front for the war between the US and Iran. But how will this affect the conflict?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How Houthi strikes on oil tankers will impact the Middle East war

events.title: Husic says Labor ‘allergic to dissent’ as he breaks ranks on key government positions at party conference
events.description: Criticism comes as prime minister frames unity as point of difference to political opponents, saying ALP chooses ‘progress over protest’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Outspoken Labor MP Ed Husic claims the party has become “allergic” to dissent and dispute, warning the unity on show at its tightly controlled national conference could backfire. The former industry minister broke ranks with the government on the first day of Labor’s 50th party conference to support a gas export tax, endorse Indigenous truth-telling and press for more criticism of Israel’s conduct in Gaza. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Husic says Labor ‘allergic to dissent’ as he breaks ranks on key government positions at party conference

events.title: Thailand: 5 soldiers killed in attack on security checkpoint
events.description: The attackers stole three AK-47 rifles, three bullet proof vests and three mobile phones from the soldiers. A low-level conflict has been simmering in Thailand's southern provinces for two decades.

events.title: US and Saudi Arabia reach a multi-billion dollar civilian nuclear deal
events.description: The United States announced that it had reached a civilian nuclear cooperation deal with Saudi Arabia. Energy Secretary Chris Wright signed the pact and a separate bilateral safeguards deal alongside Saudi Energy Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman. There have been years of discussions even under the Biden administration as part of a larger deal where Saudi Arabia would normalise relations with Israel. That fell apart after the October 7th attacks. There's likely to be pushback from allies and those concerned with safety.

events.title: Toxic gas slows efforts to rescue 13 workers trapped in India hydropower tunnel
events.description: Authorities say 13 workers are trapped inside a collapsed hydropower tunnel in India

events.title: DR Congo: Ebola outbreak still expanding, WHO sees signs of stabilization
events.description: In the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), UN health teams and partners are still racing to contain the rapidly expanding Ebola outbreak, despite ongoing insecurity in eastern areas where the virus has taken hold. As of 19 July, the DRC Government has recorded 2,423 confirmed cases, including 967 deaths and 469 recoveries.

events.title: ‘I consider it a miracle’: Pregnant woman pulled from post-quake rubble in Venezuela
events.description: When a 12-story apartment building pancaked in La Guaira, Venezuela, following two powerful earthquakes late last month, panic ensued to save people injured or trapped under the rubble.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ ‘I consider it a miracle’: Pregnant woman pulled from post-quake rubble in Venezuela

events.title: Nicaragua’s congress announces ‘work plan’ to suspend elections
events.description: US secretary of state says international community should ‘join forces’ against authoritarian Ortega regime Nicaragua’s congress, controlled by the country’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has announced a “work plan” to implement the authoritarian leader’s order that the country should no longer hold elections. The UN and the US strongly condemned Sunday’s announcement by Ortega, who has been in power since 2007 and, after a controversial constitutional change last year, now governs alongside his wife , Rosario Murillo, as “co-president”. Continue reading...

events.title: Former spy refused visa to give evidence at royal commission – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed. Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Self-reported domestic violence incidents declining in NSW but deaths and hospitalisations fail to fall New data shows self-reported domestic violence incidents in New South Wales have declined, but the number of women and children murdered or hospitalised in domestic and family violence incidents over the past eight years has not significantly budged. The decline in self-reported violence is significant because it reflects underlying levels of violence in the community, not just what is reported to police. It suggests that, despite rising reports of domestic violence to police, domestic violence in the community is not increasing and may be trending down. There’s a strong perception that violence against women is rising, which is understandable given increases in police-recorded incidents, heightened media attention and greater public awareness. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Former spy refused visa to give evidence at royal commission – as it happened

events.title: Iran war: Trump threatens bridges over Hormuz Strait attacks
events.description: US President Trump has threatened to destroy a "bridge or power plant" for each ship fired upon in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's foreign minister said any attack would "compel a powerful and decisive response."
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Trump threatens bridges over Hormuz Strait attacks

events.title: Beloved artist stabbed, killed in 'random' beach town daytime attack as police arrest 19-year-old suspect

events.title: Canada cancels joint bridge-opening celebration with US amid trade tensions
events.description: Trump’s 50% tariff threat means there will be no ribbon-cutting celebration of Gordie Howe International Bridge A planned joint US-Canada ceremony on Friday celebrating the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, is no longer expected to take place after Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods. In announcing the tariffs, the president accused Canada of unfairly discriminating against US automobiles, alcohol and dairy products, escalating tensions with one of the United States’ closest allies. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump announces new tariffs on generic drugs to take effect in 2028 – as it happened

According to the American Automobile Association (AAA), a gallon of fuel is now just over $4 – almost a dollar more than a year ago. It comes as Brent Crude , the international oil benchmark, is now up by almost 2% to $90.92 a barrel . Continue reading...

events.title: Global hunger declines again but progress remains uneven across regions
events.description: Global hunger declined for a third consecutive year in 2025 showing that progress is possible, but gains remain fragile and far too slow to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by the 2030 deadline.

events.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
events.description: Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability. There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned. Continue reading...

events.title: What to know about Ukraine's military shakeup

The protests have spread from the capital New Delhi to other large cities including Mumbai and Bengaluru. Clashes with the police have occurred in several cities as well.

events.title: Two Russian men jailed in Angola for terrorism and spying
events.description: The men were convicted over alleged plan to stage coup and organise deadly demonstrations over fuel prices last year Two Russian men have been found guilty by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying, after being accused of planning a coup and helping to organise deadly demonstrations in the southern African country last year. Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin were sentenced to eight and 11 years in jail respectively. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in Angola for cultural and business activities. Continue reading...

events.title: Former State Department official on the implications of the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal

events.title: In pictures: Wildfires ravage parts of southern France
events.description: Thousands of people were evacuated in southwest France on Thursday, as a wildfire ravaged a seaside pine forest. Hundreds of firefighters were battling the fire, which started around midday on Wednesday. FRANCE 24 looks at some of the most striking images of the fire.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ In pictures: Wildfires ravage parts of southern France

events.title: Modi vows punishment for exam fraud as 'Cockroach' protests spread
events.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has finally broken his silence on the student protest movement known as the Cockroach Janta Party. He said that his government will move to swiftly punish those responsible for the leak of answers that forced the cancellation of the country's undergrad medical exam for some 2.2 million students. That scandal sparked a youth-led revolt determined to force the ouster of the country's education minister. The protests have spread from the capital New Delhi to other large cities including Mumbai and Bengaluru.

events.title: Thousands of tourists forced to evacuate as fire rages in southwest France
events.description: Just under 12,000 people, most of them tourists, were evacuated on Thursday in southwest France as a wildfire ravaged a forest in Le Porge. The fire started midday on Wednesday, and hundreds of firefighters have been deployed to the region. France has experienced three heatwaves since May, fuelling wildfires.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Thousands of tourists forced to evacuate as fire rages in southwest France

events.title: Teachers who once dated and share child arrested after classroom argument spiraled into violence: police

events.description: Tributes are coming in for Sgt. Angel Rampersad, who was killed in action during an "enemy attack" at Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Jordan.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Is DZ Mafia a French drug cartel, an illicit 'brand', or a drug trafficking 'franchise'?
events.description: Stuart Norval is pleased to welcome Yann Bisiou, Senior Lecturer in Private Law and Criminal Law at Paul-Valéry University. The threats against the mayor of Arles illustrate how criminal networks are becoming increasingly brazen, challenging authority head-on. Rather than focusing solely on the violence, public drug policy specialist Bissiot argues that the incident exposes an institutional failure. He asserts that DZ Mafia is not a policing problem but rather the predictable consequence of a prohibition model failing to reduce a lucrative illicit drug trade.

events.title: A sailor dies after a Ukraine-bound tanker catches fire off Romania
events.description: A sailor injured when a Ukraine-bound tanker ship caught fire off Romania’s Black Sea coast has died, Romanian media reported Thursday
events.ticker_label: 💥 A sailor dies after a Ukraine-bound tanker catches fire off Romania

events.title: Travelers unite in powerful moment of silence as fallen US service member returns home
events.description: Video captured hundreds of travelers at Raleigh-Durham International Airport standing in silence during a dignified transfer. The clip gained attention online.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Travelers unite in powerful moment of silence as fallen US service member returns home

events.title: Nicaragua's parliament plans to eliminate elections to codify President Ortega's declaration
events.description: Nicaragua's congress moves to abolish elections after President Daniel Ortega declared no more elections, drawing condemnation from the international community.

events.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
events.description: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common. The central Japan cities of Gujo and Tajimi in Gifu prefecture, along with Toyota in Aichi prefecture, all hit 40C. Gujo’s 40C and Toyota’s 40.1C were all-time highs recorded at their respective weather stations. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat

events.title: U.S. nuclear power deal with Saudi Arabia faces criticism in Israel
events.description: The nuclear power deal between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia was met with alarm in Israel as it deepened concerns that Israel, a major American ally, and Trump are increasingly diverging on foreign policy and security.

events.title: Open AI models go rogue, ecape and and hack online AI sharing hub
events.description: In the world of artificial intelligence, frontier models are displaying ever-more sophisticated autonomous behaviour. Industry giant OpenAI has revealed that one of its new agentic models managed to escape what should have been an isolated testing environment to access the internet where it then proceeded to hack a popular AI sharing and testing hub as it sought answers that would help it to pass its OpenAI test. The incident, once again underlining the growing threat advanced AIs pose to cybersecurity.

events.title: Thai authorities hunt for suspects after an attack in troubled south kills 5 soldiers

events.title: Israeli UN envoy blasts Mamdani as 'clown' over call to arrest Netanyahu: 'It's a stunt'

events.title: Houthis claim attack on oil tankers as US launches more strikes on Iran
events.description: The Houthi attacks would be the first in the Red Sea since the Iran-backed group announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Houthis claim attack on oil tankers as US launches more strikes on Iran

events.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
events.description: Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull. Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu. Continue reading...

events.title: US hits Iran as Houthis claim strikes on Saudi tankers

This also comes after rebel Houthis in Yemen struck two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea, further expanding the conflict. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

events.title: U.S. signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia

events.description: The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.

events.title: India's police accused of exercising 'absolute brutality' against peaceful protesters

events.title: An accident took his legs. He adapted his motorcycle and rides on in Pakistan
events.description: A young man in Pakistan’s capital lost both legs in a road accident five years ago, but he refused to let that define his future. Today, 28-year-old Hamza Ali delivers meals across Islamabad, riding a modified motorcycle fitted with hand-operated controls. Ali appeared cheerful under the scorching sun as he told The Associated Press that he had worked for a delivery platform before the accident. Then a bus he was riding collided with a truck. “After that accident, the first six months of my life were extremely difficult,” he said. “Then I told myself, ‘Ali, you have to do something on your own.’” After nearly three years of job hunting in his hometown of Multan, where employers turned him away because of his disability, Ali moved to Islamabad in 2023 on a friend’s advice that the capital’s wider roads make it easier to ride a modified motorcycle. Using his savings, he adapted his bike and asked for his rider account to be activated. The company asked one question: whether he was...
events.ticker_label: 💥 An accident took his legs. He adapted his motorcycle and rides on in Pakistan

events.title: Total solar eclipse books out hotels two years in advance in New Zealand city
events.description: Dunedin will be the focal point, having last experienced such an eclipse in 1163 – before any humans reached New Zealand Hotels are booking out two years in advance in the New Zealand city of Dunedin as it prepares for the region’s first total solar eclipse in more than 850 years. At 4.17pm on 22 July 2028, the moon will pass in front of the sun, casting a roughly 100km-wide shadowy band across parts of southern New Zealand. Everywhere within that band – from tourist hotspot Queenstown in the west to Dunedin city in the east – will be plunged into daytime darkness for 2 minutes and 51 seconds. Continue reading...

events.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
events.description: Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby. Researchers from Project Ceti (the Cetacean Translation Initiative), a non-profit research organisation working to decode sperm whale communication, spent four years tuning in on the calls of 15 sperm whales off the coast of Dominica. Continue reading...

events.description: Germany is keen to boost its economy. On his third trip to Africa, Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul is visiting Nigeria and South Africa to promote greater cooperation.

events.title: Lenacapavir will slow HIV infections if there's enough of it and it reaches at-risk groups
events.description: Lenacapavir is one injection, twice a year. It's a big improvement for people in high-risk groups, currently taking a pill a day against HIV infection. But its long-term impact depends on its reaching those very people.

events.title: EU fines Google €890 million over competition rules
events.description: The European Commission said it found that Google had preferenced its search engine and app store in ways that unfairly harmed competition.

events.title: Germany news: FM Wadephul wraps up Africa tour in South Africa
events.description: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul's Africa tour ends today in South Africa, Germany's largest trade partner on the continent. Follow DW for the latest.

events.title: Who is Ukraine's new military chief?
events.description: Mykhailo Drapatyi has been made commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces. His appointment follows a cabinet reshuffle that ousted defense minister Fedorov.

events.title: How the student 'Cockroach' movement grew its influence across India
events.description: Young Indians are keeping up the pressure - continuing their demands for the Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan - to step down. This as their online satirical group the "Cockroach People's Party" grows in stature and support. It won millions of followers on social media and tens of thousands of sign-ups since its launch in May.

events.title: US trial date set for Venezuela's ousted president Maduro
events.description: Venezuela's captured president Nicolas Maduro and his wife Cilia Flores appeared in a US court today over drugs trafficking charges.

events.title: Remains of 22 more infants found at former mother and baby home
events.description: The remains of a further 22 infants have been found at the site of a former mother and baby home in Ireland, investigators have said.

events.title: UK considered suspending trade agreement with Israel
events.description: The UK considered pausing its trade agreement with Israel, having undertaken an impact assessment on suspension last summer.

events.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead at Paris home
events.description: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein has been found dead at his home in Paris.

events.title: 2,000 volunteers join a painstaking effort to restore massive prehistoric skeleton
events.description: More than 2,000 volunteers are helping Polish scientists restore a Mastodonsaurus skeleton

events.title: Ukraine overhauls its military top brass for the fight against Russia
events.description: Ukraine is continuing its fight against Russia with new military leadership

events.title: Louvre reopens Apollo Gallery after a brazen heist, but no jewels on display
events.description: The Louvre's Apollo gallery is reopening to the public for the first time since last year's crown jewels heist

events.title: FBI Director Patel returns looted cultural artifacts to Indonesian President Prabowo
events.description: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto has received a collection of repatriated cultural artifacts from FBI Director Kash Patel during a meeting in Jakarta

events.title: Vatican defends secrecy in sensitive abuse case involving ex-Jesuit artist Marko Rupnik
events.description: The Vatican has confirmed that a church trial against a famous ex-Jesuit priest accused of spiritual and sexual abuse of women is ongoing

events.title: Voters want major change amid economic and political discontent and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Arkansas cop tragically dies saving fiancée's life just hours after proposing
events.description: An Arkansas police officer proposed to his fiancée hours before drowning in Elk River while rescuing her from the current during a canoeing trip with friends.

events.title: Border K-9 sniffs out 99 pounds of prohibited meat, plants and monitor lizard in suitcase from Thailand
events.description: K-9 Maisy discovered 50 pounds of raw meat, mangoes and 16 pounds of Bengal monitor lizard in luggage from Thailand, prompting a wildlife seizure.

events.title: Watch: Missouri suspect dives headfirst off bridge in wild bid to evade deputies
events.description: Video captures a Missouri man sprinting across a bridge and diving headfirst into Table Rock Lake after leading Stone County deputies on a chase.

events.title: Manhunt underway after masked gunman allegedly shoots NYC cab driver in face during overnight robbery
events.description: A masked gunman allegedly shot a 21-year-old livery cab driver in the face during an attempted robbery in Brooklyn before fleeing with cash, according to police.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Manhunt underway after masked gunman allegedly shoots NYC cab driver in face during overnight robbery

events.title: Sheriff investigating Nancy Guthrie disappearance hunting suspect in separate Tucson home invasion
events.description: A manhunt is underway in Pima County after a woman was sexually assaulted during a home invasion about 30 miles from where Nancy Guthrie vanished.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sheriff investigating Nancy Guthrie disappearance hunting suspect in separate Tucson home invasion

events.title: ‘Hero' mom dies after using last strength to save 4-year-old son from Florida river
events.description: Nicole Grayson, 43, jumped into the St. Johns River at Ed Stone Park to save her 4-year-old son, using all her strength to keep him above water.

events.title: Trump flexes power in Arizona primaries as Democrats dodge far-left upset and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: $290M cocaine haul found ‘skillfully concealed’ in massive banana shipment
events.description: Italian officials said the 1,700-pound haul of cocaine, hidden among banana pallets, could have generated roughly $290 million on the street.

events.title: Sinaloa Cartel boss 'El Mayo' sentenced to life, ordered to pay $15B in drug trafficking profits
events.description: Sinaloa Cartel co-founder Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada Garcia received a life sentence and was ordered to forfeit $15 billion in drug trafficking profits.

events.title: Andy Burnham takes over as UK prime minister, calls for 'new political model'
events.description: Andy Burnham enters Downing Street vowing to shift power away from London, but his past criticism of Trump could create early friction with Washington.

events.title: Steve Forbes urges Trump to keep the Strait of Hormuz open at all costs
events.description: Publisher Steve Forbes calls for maximum pressure on Tehran regime, urges Trump administration to keep the Strait of Hormuz open as a non-negotiable imperative.
